decisions.support_rationale: All three experiences explicitly occur within the software delivery domain, confirming the scope.
Both support cases demonstrate that exposing a small subset of traffic (canary/cohorts) led to the early detection of specific defects (regression/language defect).
The payments canary experience explicitly states the outcome that the old version remained available after the rollback of the canary cohort.
The counterexample (atomic_schema) confirms that lack of coexistence (backward compatibility) causes failure, while support cases imply compatibility allowed the old version to remain operational.
The evidence describes scenarios matching the condition: incremental rollout (5% traffic, cohorts) where backward compatibility was maintained (old version available).
In both support cases, the first exposed group (canary/cohort two) successfully revealed a critical defect (regression/language defect).
Evidence shows that in both successful cases, the rest of the system (non-canary traffic, remaining tenants) was not affected or moved due to the defect found in the subset.
The payments canary outcome explicitly states that only the canary cohort needed rollback and the old version remained available, supporting the claim.
The evidence explicitly states 'Only the canary cohort needed rollback', directly matching the claim.
The outcome 'the old version remained available' in the payments canary case supports the criterion that the old version remains operational.

The atomic_schema counterexample describes a partial rollout (incremental) without compatibility that created incompatible records and required more recovery work, representing a system-wide issue/outage scenario.

The counterexample notes 'more recovery work' was needed due to incompatible records; while 'rollback of old version' isn't explicitly stated, the incompatibility implies the old state could not be simply retained without significant remediation, fitting the falsification condition of forced major rollback/recovery.

The evidence states 'more recovery work' and 'incompatible records' but does not explicitly confirm the entire system became 'unavailable'; the severity is implied but not strictly proven as total unavailability.

The evidence mentions 'more recovery work' but does not explicitly state that the 'old version is rolled back'; the specific action of rolling back the old version is an inference not directly supported by the text.
